command.text: find /testbed -type f -name '*.php' -print0 | xargs -0 grep -E -c '[[:alnum:]]' | awk '{s+=$1} END{print s}'

raw_output_payload.content: find /testbed -type f -name '*.php' -print0 | xargs -0 grep -E -c '[[:alnum:]]' | awk '{s+=$1} END{print s}'
raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need bash command to count lines with alphanumeric symbols in all .php files under /testbed. Use grep -E -c? Actually count lines that contain at least one alphanumeric character. Use grep -E -o? We can use grep -E -c '[[:alnum:]]' but that counts per file. Need total. Use grep -E -c? Actually grep -E -c counts per file. Instead use grep -E -o? Wait: we can use grep -E -c '[[:alnum:]]' file | awk sum. Or simpler: grep -E -c '[[:alnum:]]' $(find /testbed -name '*.php') | awk '{s+=$1} END{print s}'. But grep -c counts lines per file. That works. So command: grep -E -c '[[:alnum:]]' $(find /testbed -type f -name '*.php') | awk '{s+=$1} END{print s}'. But need to handle many files; use xargs. Use find ... -print0 | xargs -0 grep -E -c '[[:alnum:]]' | awk... But grep -c with multiple files prints each file count. So final: find /testbed -type f -name '*.php' -print0 | xargs -0 grep -E -c '[[:alnum:]]' | awk '{s+=$1} END{print s}'.

task.query: Counts total number of only lines with alphanumeric symbols in all *.php files in the /testbed folder and subfolders.
task.gold: find /testbed -name '*.php' | xargs cat | awk '/[a-zA-Z0-9]/ {i++} END{print i}'
